title: Retry backend deploy: real heartbeat data + robust fetchSignals structured response

requirements.text:
Update `backend/main.mo` `getHeartbeatData(accountId)` to return real, non-hardcoded data derived from existing canister state while preserving the same public method name and access control.
- Look up the caller’s `BotProfile` from `botProfiles` and use it to set `botStatus` (`#ACTIVE` only when a bot profile exists and `uplinkStatus == true`, otherwise `#SUSPENDED`) and `lastHeartbeatAt` (use `botProfile.lastHeartbeat` when present, otherwise `0`).
- Determine `verifiedLicense` by looking up the caller’s `UserProfile` in `userProfiles`, reading `accountId`, then checking `licenses.get(accountId)` and returning `true` only if a license exists and `active == true`.
- Keep `cycles` retrieval constrained to the current Motoko environment: if a cycle-balance API is not available, keep `cycles = 0` and include the exact comment `// TODO: Replace with actual cycle balance API when available.` (do not introduce other cycle APIs).
- Populate `cyclesWarning : ?Text` when `cycles < 500_000_000_000`, otherwise `null`.
acceptanceCriteria: Calling `getHeartbeatData` as an authenticated user returns a `HeartbeatData` where `botStatus`, `lastHeartbeatAt`, and `verifiedLicense` reflect the caller’s stored `BotProfile`/`UserProfile`/`licenses` state (no placeholder values).
If the caller has no `BotProfile`, `botStatus` is `#SUSPENDED` and `lastHeartbeatAt` is `0`.
If the caller has no `UserProfile.accountId`, or no license entry, or the license is inactive, then `verifiedLicense` is `false`; otherwise `true`.
`cyclesWarning` is `null` when `cycles >= 500_000_000_000` and is `?Text` when below that threshold.
No other public endpoints’ signatures or behaviors are modified.

requirements.text:
Harden `backend/main.mo` `fetchSignals()` success-path audit logging so it never traps when creating the response snippet:
- Keep returning the structured success result shape `#ok({ statusCode : Nat; body : Text; timestamp : Int })`.
- When creating the audit-log snippet, include the full response body if `response.size() <= 100`, otherwise include only the first 100 characters.
- Ensure any slicing/substring logic uses bounds-safe end indices (no out-of-range slicing).
acceptanceCriteria: When `OutCall.httpGetRequest` returns a response body shorter than 100 characters, `fetchSignals()` returns `#ok(...)` and adds an audit entry without trapping.
When the response body is longer than 100 characters, the audit log `details` field starts with the exact prefix `Signals fetched: ` followed by exactly the first 100 characters of the response.
The returned `#ok` payload contains `statusCode = 200`, the full `body` text, and `timestamp` set from `Time.now()` (as an `Int`).
Existing `#err` behaviors and messages for unauthorized access, missing bot profile, missing bot URL, and invalid URL remain unchanged.

requirements.text:
Fix `backend/main.mo` `saveCallerUserProfile` bot-id indexing logic so `botIdIndex` correctly removes the previous `bot_id` mapping when the caller changes their `bot_id`.
- Capture the prior profile (if any) before overwriting `userProfiles`.
- After saving the new profile, if the old profile had a different `bot_id`, remove the old mapping from `botIdIndex`.
- If the new profile has `bot_id = ?Text`, set/overwrite `botIdIndex[newBotId] = caller`.
- If the new profile has `bot_id = null`, do not add a new index entry (and remove the old one if it existed).
acceptanceCriteria: Updating a user profile from `bot_id = ?"A"` to `bot_id = ?"B"` results in `botIdIndex` no longer containing `A` and containing `B -> caller`.
Updating a user profile from `bot_id = ?"A"` to `bot_id = null` results in `botIdIndex` no longer containing `A`.
Existing access-control behavior and the public signature of `saveCallerUserProfile` remain unchanged.

constraints: Backend must remain a single Motoko actor in `backend/main.mo` (no additional backend services).
Do not modify any other backend endpoints beyond what is necessary to implement `getHeartbeatData`, `fetchSignals` robustness, and `saveCallerUserProfile` botIdIndex correctness.
Keep the exact comment `// TODO: Replace with actual cycle balance API when available.` when `cycles` is forced to `0`.
User-facing text in traps/errors/toasts must be English.
nonGoals: Do not implement or modify Stripe actor wiring (`backend/stripe/stripe.mo` integration) as part of this retry.
Do not add new frontend features; frontend changes related to these backend shapes are already tracked as existing features.
Do not implement full Ed25519 signature verification in `check_uplink` (it should remain TODO).
